deployment_question: How does the model transfer to protein families or remote homologs not represented in training?
definition: Hold out protein families, superfamilies, or remote-homology groups rather than only enforcing a pairwise identity threshold.

split_operator.spectra_mapping: targets an extreme sequence/function novelty point beyond near-homology exclusion

implementation_recipe: Assign proteins to family or superfamily annotations.
Hold out complete families or superfamilies.
Check residual structure or remote-homology similarity to training proteins.
quality_gates: Use curated family annotations where possible.
Report both sequence identity and family overlap.
Avoid mixing proteins from the same functional family across splits.
failure_modes: Family annotations may be incomplete or inconsistent.
Remote structural similarity can remain despite sequence family holdout.

citations.title: GRIMM: Genetic Stratification for Inference in Molecular Modeling
citations.evidence: Introduces genetic stratification to prevent similar enzyme sequences from appearing in both train and test.

citations.title: A comprehensive system for evaluation of remote sequence similarity detection
citations.evidence: Builds evaluation sets for remote sequence similarity detection with multiple perspectives.
